name: ORMAT TECHNOLOGIES, INC.
business: Ormat Technologies is a vertically integrated renewable energy company primarily engaged in the geothermal power business. It operates in three segments: Electricity (develops, builds, owns, and operates geothermal, solar PV, and recovered energy power plants and sells electricity under long-term PPAs), Product (designs, manufactures, and sells equipment for geothermal and recovered energy electricity generation and provides EPC services), and Energy Storage (owns and operates grid-connected battery energy storage systems).

exposures.sentence: We are a leading vertically integrated company primarily engaged in the geothermal power business.

we are expanding into different complementary energy solutions, including stand alone utility scale energy storage services and solar PV generation (including hybrid geothermal and solar PV as well as solar plus energy storage).

In the Energy Storage segment, we own and operate grid-connected, stand alone In Front of the Meter (IFM) BESS facilities, which provide capacity, energy and ancillary services directly to the electric grid.

We leverage our core capabilities, proprietary technologies, and global presence to expand our activities in conventional geothermal development, recovered energy generation and emerging geothermal technologies, including piloting of new EGS technologies.

Demand for electricity generated from geothermal and other renewable resources in the United States has increased due to the need for reliable baseload power and the growing energy requirements of data centers.

Areas of interest include advanced geothermal technologies, energy storage solutions, renewable and waste heat recovery technologies (including industrial-scale heat pumps, heat engines and other clean power generation solutions), and nuclear fission small modular reactor (SMR) deployment technologies.
we own and operate grid-connected, stand alone In Front of the Meter (IFM) BESS facilities, which provide capacity, energy and ancillary services directly to the electric grid.
Our customers are investor-owned, publicly owned utilities, and, more recently, data centers operators, Hyperscalers, public and private corporations and CCAs.

catalysts.event: COD of Arrowleaf hybrid solar-plus-storage project
catalysts.sentence: In December 2025, we reached the COD for Arrowleaf, our first hybrid solar-plus-storage project, consisting of approximately 42MW of solar generation capacity and 35MW/140MWh of energy storage.

In January 2026, we acquired Hoku, a recently built operational solar-plus-storage facility on the Big Island of Hawaii, from Innergex Renewable Energy Inc. for total cash consideration of $80.5 million.
In January 2026, we made a $25 million investment in Sage Geosystems Inc. ( Sage ) as part of Sage s Series B financing round.

catalysts.event: Award of Telaga Ranu concession in Indonesia
catalysts.sentence: In January 2026, we were awarded the Telaga Ranu geothermal working area concession in Indonesia following a competitive tender process.

In January 2026, we entered into a new 20-year PPA with Switch, Inc., a leading provider of data center infrastructure, pursuant to which Switch will purchase approximately 13MW of carbon-free geothermal capacity from our Salt Wells geothermal power plant.

catalysts.event: PPA with Google for up to 150MW
catalysts.sentence: In February 2026, we entered into a long-term geothermal portfolio PPA to supply up to 150MW of new geothermal capacity to support Google's data center s energy needs, through NV Energy's Clean Transition Tariff program.

catalysts.event: COD of Lower Rio energy storage facility
catalysts.sentence: In September 2025, we successfully commenced the commercial operations of our 60MW/120MWh Lower Rio energy storage facility, located in Texas.

catalysts.event: Signing of GEECA agreements with PLN
catalysts.sentence: In August 2025, we signed two Geothermal Exploration and Energy Conversion Agreements ( GEECA ), a novel form of power purchase agreement, with Perusahaan Listrik Negara ( PLN ), each covering up to 20 MW of geothermal capacity each in Songa Wayaua and Atadei located in Indonesia.

catalysts.event: 25-year PPA extension with SCPPA for Heber 1
catalysts.sentence: In August 2025, we announced the signing of a 25-year extension to our existing power purchase agreement with SCPPA, for the 52MW from Heber 1 geothermal facility.

In July 2025, we entered into loan agreements with a consortium of French banks pursuant to which we will borrow up to approximately 99.8 million aggregate principal amount in connection with our new Bouillante geothermal power plant in Guadeloupe.

In July 2025, we entered into a tax partnership agreement with a private investor, under which the private investor paid approximately $77.1 million for the tax benefits related to the Heber 1&2 Geothermal power plants.

catalysts.event: Acquisition of Blue Mountain geothermal plant
catalysts.sentence: In June 2025, we closed the acquisition of the Blue Mountain geothermal power plant from Cyrq Energy.

catalysts.event: Hybrid Tax Equity partnership with Morgan Stanley
catalysts.sentence: In May 2025, we announced the signing of a $62.0 million Hybrid Tax Equity partnership with Morgan Stanley Renewables, Inc.

catalysts.event: COD of Ijen geothermal power plant
catalysts.sentence: In February 2025, we announced the successful COD for the Ijen geothermal power plant that is owned jointly with PT Medco Power Indonesia.

catalysts.event: Award of tolling agreements in Israel
catalysts.sentence: In February 2025, we won a tender issued by the Israeli Electricity Authority and have been awarded two separate 15-year tolling agreements for two Energy Storage facilities.
In January 2025, we announced the signing of a 10-year PPA with Calpine Energy Solutions, one of North America s largest energy suppliers.

tamClaims.claim: Total installed geothermal power generation capacity stood at 17,144 MW, an increase of 271 MW in 2025.
tamClaims.sentence: At the end of 2025, the total installed geothermal power generation capacity stood at 17,144 MW, an increase of 271 MW in 2025.

tamClaims.claim: Wood Mackenzie forecasts 93 GW of energy storage will be added to the grid between 2025-2029.
tamClaims.sentence: Wood Mackenzie is forecasting that 93 GW of energy storage will be added to the grid between 2025-2029, with the majority expected to be grid-scale installations.

tamClaims.claim: Indonesia has an estimated geothermal potential of 23 GW, the largest in the world, but only 2.7 GW utilized.
tamClaims.sentence: Indonesia has an estimated geothermal potential of 23GW, the largest in the world, but has only utilized about 2.7 GW (i.e. 13%) of this potential.
